title: Start an agent in one command
description: Launch a CLI agent in a VSCode terminal with no setup or API key.

title: Detect missing or signed-out CLI
description: Tell the user when the CLI is not installed or not signed in, with a fix.

title: Multi-turn interactive terminal mode
description: Drive codex as a live TUI in a VSCode terminal: multi-turn steering with pause/resume in one session, via a file mailbox.

title: Coarse live status
description: Show launching/ready/busy/awaiting-input/done from the mailbox lifecycle.

title: Awaiting-input notification
description: Notify when an agent is parked waiting on the user.

title: Send a task from the palette
description: Delegate a task or prompt to a running agent from the command palette.

title: Stop an agent
description: Stop any agent at any time to stay in control.

title: Full conversation in a terminal tab
description: Everything the agent does is visible and takeover-able in a normal terminal (also satisfies US7.3).

title: Concurrent sessions in a sidebar
description: Run several agents at once, listed with status in a sidebar.

title: One worktree/branch per agent
description: Each agent works in its own git worktree so none overwrite others.

title: Review diff before merge
description: Review exactly what an agent changed before it is merged.

title: Per-account concurrency cap
description: Configurable low-default cap on simultaneous sessions per account.

title: Define roles
description: Roles with responsibilities, permissions, workflow position, and definition of done.

title: Starter role templates
description: A working team of well-engineered role templates out of the box.

title: Give a worker a soul
description: Personality, values, taste, quality bar, and style for consistent, recognizable work.

title: Souls as versionable files
description: Read, edit, version-control, and share souls as portable, non-locked assets.

title: Compile harness to CLI files
description: Compile role + soul + conventions + briefing into each CLI's native instruction files.

title: Preview harness material
description: Preview exactly what harness a worker receives before its session starts.

title: Persistent worker memory
description: Keep lessons learned so the same feedback never has to be given twice.

title: Worker track record
description: Record and show tasks completed, rework rate, and review pass rate.

title: Tag tasks by type and skill
description: Auto-suggested, editable task tags so matching has real signal to match on.

title: Assign the best-suited worker
description: Score model strengths, role fit, and track record to route by quality.

title: Explain why a worker was chosen
description: Show the scoring breakdown behind each assignment to trust and tune it.

title: Override and learn from it
description: Override any assignment and feed the override back into future matching.

title: Switch matching policy
description: Choose best-suited, fixed provider priority, quota-first, or manual.

title: Score suitability with an LLM, deterministic fallback
description: Deterministic Mediator consults an API-key LLM Reasoner (over HTTP, OpenAI-compatible adapter) for suitability scoring; later consumers: standups (US6.4.1) and retro-replan (US6.5.1/2). HTTP allowed behind the Reasoner port only; workers stay pty; never subscription auth. Research-stage, no spec yet.

title: Register multiple accounts
description: Named accounts per provider with isolated CLI config directories.

title: See sign-in status
description: One place to catch an expired login before it fails a task mid-sprint.

title: Secrets in SecretStorage
description: Keep sensitive profile data in VSCode SecretStorage, never in settings.json.

title: Passive quota tracking
description: Read remaining headroom per account from the CLIs' own session logs, no extra calls.

title: Status-bar headroom meter
description: See remaining capacity per account at a glance.

title: Near-limit warning
description: Warn before an account is cut off mid-task.

title: PM worker drafts backlog
description: A PM-role worker drafts and refines backlog items, with your approval.

title: Capacity-aware planning
description: Sprint planning considers remaining quota so commitments match what subscriptions deliver.

title: Kanban board
description: Backlog / in progress / review / done with the worker on each task, run from the IDE.

title: Enforced review gate
description: A dev worker's diff must be reviewed by a QA/PO worker or you before it proceeds.

title: Rework loop on rejection
description: Rejected reviews return to the same worker with the reviewer's feedback attached.

title: Standup digest
description: On-demand done / doing / blocked digest across all workers since the last summary.

title: Retro writes soul lessons
description: Retro turns what went wrong into lessons written into the responsible workers' souls.

title: Retro triggers replanning
description: Retro outcomes replan the remaining backlog so the team course-corrects.

title: Human-plausible cadence
description: Keep session concurrency and input cadence within a human power user's range.

title: Per-provider kill-switch
description: Instantly disable one provider leg if its policy changes.

title: No hidden traffic
description: Everything Skynet sends to an agent is visible in the terminal.

title: CI integration-test leg
description: xvfb integration leg for test:integration, already a TODO in ci.yml.

title: Adapter contract tests
description: Tests runnable against live CLIs to catch upstream breaking changes early.

title: Harness evaluation suite
description: Replayable benchmark tasks to measure whether a role/soul/harness change improves output.

title: Marketplace release
description: vsce packaging, README + demo, CHANGELOG discipline; telemetry only if opt-in.
